data.description:
## Why

Audit Maintainability finding #3 (MEDIUM). The codebase reads 80 distinct REBAR_* environment variables across 133 os.environ/getenv call sites; only ~42 sit in the three intended config modules (config.py, llm/config.py, review_bot/config.py) — the rest are scattered, worst in the reconciler. There is no single artifact enumerating what can be set, so discovering the config surface requires grep, and a new env var can ship undocumented. The repo already solved this exact problem shape twice: the derived prompt index and the generated TypedDicts, both with regenerate-and-diff CI drift gates.

## What

Add a generator that scans the tree for REBAR_[A-Z_]+ reads and emits a canonical registry table (name, default, reading module, doc line, deprecated-alias status), committed under docs/ (e.g. docs/env-vars.md or a docs/config-reference section). Wire a CI drift gate in test.yml: regenerate and diff, fail on drift — same pattern as the prompt index. Deprecated aliases (REBAR_PUSH, REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_RECONCILE_LIVE) are marked as such by the generator.

## Scope

In: the generator script (scripts/), the committed registry doc, the CI gate step, one-line doc-comment fixes where the generator finds an undocumented var. Out: consolidating scattered os.environ reads into the config modules (a follow-on refactor this registry makes safe to plan), adding/removing any env var.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A committed, generated registry lists every REBAR_* var read anywhere in src/ with reading module and deprecation status
- [ ] CI drift gate: regenerating the registry and diffing is a build step in test.yml; drift fails the build
- [ ] Generator handles the known alias pairs and marks deprecated names
- [ ] Adding a new REBAR_ read without regenerating the registry demonstrably fails CI (covered by the gate test or a unit test on the generator)
- [ ] README/docs pointer so the registry is discoverable
data.title: Generated REBAR_* env-var registry with a CI drift gate
